codeReview.summary: Comprehensive implementation of the complete Π lane UI. All 5 required sub-features are present and well-structured: (1) bridge cards with connection type badge, strength bar, realism/fear/public-benefit scores, and semantic similarity from browser embedding; (2) action cards with urgency indicator, title, description, and interactive checklist; (3) comparison panel with side-by-side global/local thread viewing; (4) federation comparison sub-panel with 3 placeholder peers; (5) P→R→N→Π→A feedback loop SVG diagram. The connection detection engine (connections.ts) is cleanly separated from the rendering layer (PiLaneConnections.tsx), uses proper TypeScript types throughout (no `any`), handles errors with `catch (err: unknown)` pattern per project conventions, and includes 42 new/updated tests covering both logic and UI interactions. The implementation fulfills all 6 validation contract items (VAL-PI-001 through VAL-PI-005, VAL-CROSS-004).

issues.description: formatRelativeTime is duplicated — this same function exists in EtaThreadCard.tsx (line 19) and MuThreadCard.tsx (line 98). The mission AGENTS.md coding conventions explicitly state: 'Extract common utilities (formatRelativeTime, lane-tag mappings) to src/ui/utils/'. No utils/ directory has been created and the duplication continues.
Bridge cards are silently capped at 20 via bridges.slice(0, 20) with no UI indication that additional connections exist. If >20 connections are detected, users have no way to know some are hidden.
Using array index as React key (key={idx}) for checklist items. Acceptable since the list is static and never reordered, but technically a React anti-pattern.
Compare Threads and Federation View toggle buttons lack aria-expanded attributes to communicate open/close state to screen readers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md states 'Extract common utilities (formatRelativeTime, lane-tag mappings) to src/ui/utils/' but no such directory exists and 3 components (EtaThreadCard, MuThreadCard, PiLaneConnections) each define their own formatRelativeTime. The convention is documented but has never been enforced, creating tech debt across multiple features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line 30: 'Shared UI utils: Extract common utilities (formatRelativeTime, lane-tag mappings) to src/ui/utils/'. Grep shows 3 copies: EtaThreadCard.tsx:19, MuThreadCard.tsx:98, PiLaneConnections.tsx:112.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker suggested the frontend-worker skill should mention that Postgres may not be running in dev, with fallback verification approaches. This is a valid observation — the skill's step 5 says to 'Ensure threat-radar-mcp is running on 9001' but doesn't acknowledge that Postgres may be down, which would prevent the MCP server from starting and block visual verification with real data.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The frontend-worker skill could mention that Postgres may not be running in the dev environment, suggesting fallback verification approaches when the full backend stack isn't available.' Worker used mock data in tests + agent-browser screenshot showing placeholder text since backend wasn't running.

summary: PASS. The pi-lane-full feature is a thorough, well-structured implementation covering all 5 required sub-features and fulfilling all 6 validation contract items. The connection detection engine is cleanly separated from the UI layer with comprehensive test coverage (42 tests). No blocking issues found. Four non-blocking issues noted: duplicated formatRelativeTime utility, silent bridge card cap at 20, index-based React keys on static list, and missing aria-expanded attributes on toggles. Two shared state observations: the documented but unenforced utils/ extraction convention, and a skill gap around Postgres availability in dev.
